channel.title: Short Essay on jqknono Blogs
channel.description: Recent content in Short Essay on jqknono Blogs

item.title: WeChat Input Method voice supports removing filler words alu!
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description: Archive of a Xiaohongshu short post about WeChat Input Method voice supporting removal of filler words alu!

item.title: Learn Product Design from Big Tech
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description: Page 1: As the repayment due date approaches, the most important action is ‘make repayment’ rather than ‘repay with ease’, yet the visual emphasis is on ‘repay with ease’. ‘Repay with ease’ actually refers to installment repayment. Page 2: What matters is ‘how much is the interest’ rather than ‘how much interest is reduced’…

item.title: I understand that we can no longer casually criticize companies
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description: Although some netizens’ posts do have the problem of the blind men feeling the elephant, it shouldn’t go as far as forbidding the squirrel from saying the water is deep. Comparison posts often have losers and winners, and I wonder if losers will be banned in the future. Some manufacturers really overhype, or use obscure qualifiers in their rankings, which easily misleads ordinary consumers. Previously, product criticisms were shared based on personal experience to help others avoid pitfalls, but…

item.title: One factor in architecture rot is compromising on development cost
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description: Characteristics of rotten architecture include: lots of special-case handling, compatibility handling, severe coupling, and being too burdened to reform. All architectures gradually rot. A rotten architecture makes it hard to develop new features, fix bugs, and a small change affects everything, making testing and QA very difficult as well…

item.title: Is there a quantitative strategy suitable for the A-share market?
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description: I’m still a bit confused. If I had a strategy that could make money, I would definitely go all in with a loan and would never easily share that strategy. So what do all those people online who share strategies want?
